sections.paragraph:
Κάϊν, ὁ indecl. (קַיִן; W-H. Καίν; LXX; En 22:7; Philo; TestBenj 7:5; TestAbr A 13 p. 92, 6 [Stone p. 32]; ApcMos, Philo; Just., D. 99, 3.—In Jos., Ant. 1, 52; 65f; 57 Κάις, Κάιος, Κάιν) Cain, Adam’s son (Gen 4:1ff) Hb 11:4; 1J 3:12; Jd 11; 1 Cl 4:1ff.—NDahl, in Apophoreta (Haenchen Festschr.) ’64, 70–84; PBretscher, Cain, Come Home! ’76; JReeves, Heralds of That Good Realm ’96, 1100f, n. 89 on Cain’s patrimony in tradition. S. also Ἅβελ.—TW.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

W-H. W-H. = BWestcott/FHort—List 1, beg.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 496
